data.title: A newly created multi-signature wallet withdrew nearly 400 million ALT from Binance 14 minutes ago, equivalent to about 19.82 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created multi-signature wallet withdrew 399,999,000 ALTs, equivalent to US$19.82 million, from Binance 14 minutes ago. The wallet may belong to AltLayer. Yesterday, the wallet withdrew 1,000 ALTs and destroyed them.

data.title: AlphaX releases latest real-time data, AI trading strategy win rate and profit remain at a high level
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official disclosure, after AlphaX was launched on BNB Smart Chain (BSC), it completed a two-month real-time test from December 2024 to January 2025. The entire transaction was independently executed by AI without human intervention, and the data performance is worthy of attention.
Test data shows that AlphaX's winning rate and income in the execution of AI trading strategies remain at a high level:
 •  December 2024: ROI (return on investment) +18.21%, winning rate 89.47%
 •  January 2025: ROI (return on investment) +16.00%, winning rate 92.86%
As the first AI trading model based on the Rena framework under the DeAgentAI ecosystem, AlphaX combines on-chain data analysis, strategy optimization and automated execution to run through AI-driven trading logic in DeFi trading scenarios. Judging from the current real-time data, AI trading not only has high market adaptability, but also the stability of strategy execution is continuously improving.
At present, AlphaX has been tested in the multi-chain DeFi ecosystem, and has cooperated with projects such as Movement, Bitlayer, ai16z, base, GoPlus, etc. to further optimize AI trading strategies and promote the application of DeFAI ecology. In the future, the team will continue to explore the expansion scenarios of AI in intelligent trading and strengthen the optimization of market analysis and risk management.
The market influence of AI trading is gradually expanding, and AlphaX also provides further data support for the feasibility of AI trading agents. More test data and real-time progress, continue to pay attention.

data.title: DePIN Becomes Key Topic In U.S. Crypto Policy
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on February 6, the Washington Blockchain Association announced that DePIN has officially become a significant topic in U.S. crypto policy-making. Larry Pang, head of the IoTeX ecosystem, has been elected as the co-chair of the association's DePIN working group. He will lead the group alongside Jacob Hample from the Filecoin Foundation to advance policy development in the DePIN sector within the U.S. crypto industry.

Ron Hammond, the association's director of government relations and a former House staffer, highlighted the lack of clear crypto regulations in the U.S., which has left businesses in a gray area and sometimes forced them to relocate to other countries. Hammond has been actively seeking solutions to help the U.S. maintain its competitiveness in the global crypto industry.

As regulatory clarity and industry standards become more defined, IoTeX is leveraging its expertise in the DePIN field to promote the mainstream adoption of this technology both in the U.S. and globally.

data.title: Significant Withdrawal of WIF Tokens from CEX Wallets
data.description: According to Odaily, data from The Data Nerd indicates that over the past 24 hours, several wallets have withdrawn a total of 22.26 million WIF tokens from centralized exchanges (CEX), valued at approximately $18.02 million. Specifically, the wallet starting with G1pif withdrew 2.68 million WIF, equivalent to about $2.1 million. The wallet beginning with A3kTT extracted 6.5 million WIF, worth around $5.18 million. Additionally, the D2Noa wallet withdrew 4.08 million WIF, approximately $3.38 million in value, while the 4x3M8 wallet took out 9 million WIF, valued at about $7.36 million.

data.title: OKX Web3 wallet will support Berachain mainnet
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, OKX Web3 wallet will support Berachain mainnet. At that time, users can select Berachain network in the wallet support network and perform operations such as querying assets, transfers, and on-chain dapp interactions.
It is reported that Berachain is a high-performance EVM-compatible blockchain built on liquidity proof consensus. Liquidity proof is a novel consensus mechanism that aims to coordinate network incentives and establish strong synergy between Berachain validators and project ecosystems.

data.title: New Malware SparkCat Targets Cryptocurrency Wallets
data.description:
According to Foresight News, cybersecurity firm Kaspersky Labs has identified a new malware activity named SparkCat, discovered at the end of 2024. The attackers aim to use Optical Character Recognition (OCR) to steal mnemonic phrases, allowing them to regain access to cryptocurrency wallets and potentially take full control to further steal funds. Applications infected with this malware have been found on both Google Play and the App Store. It remains unclear whether these apps were compromised through a supply chain attack or if developers intentionally embedded the Trojan. Over 242,000 downloads of the infected applications have been recorded from Google Play alone. SparkCat has been active since March 2024.

Kaspersky Labs advises users to remove any infected applications from their devices and refrain from using them until patches addressing the malicious features are released. Additionally, users are cautioned against storing screenshots containing sensitive information.

data.title: In the past 24 hours, multiple wallets have withdrawn a total of 22.26 million WIF from CEX, equivalent to approximately US$18.02 million
Cumberland has withdrawn 62,381 ETH from exchanges in the past two days, equivalent to about $174 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, in the past two days, Cumberland withdrew 62,381 ETH (US$174 million) from the exchange and transferred it to Coinbase Prime.

data.title: CFTC fines EmpiresX founder $130 million for crypto fraud
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) announced that a U.S. federal court has ordered more than $130 million in fines and compensation to the Brazilian founders of EmpiresX, an illegal cryptocurrency investment platform. On February 4, U.S. District Court Judge Cecilia Altonaga of the Southern District of Florida imposed permanent injunctions, financial penalties, and other legal actions on EmpiresX founders Emerson Pires, Flavio Goncalves, and their partner Joshua Nicholas. The case was originally filed on June 30, 2022, and a default judgment was ultimately made because the defendants failed to respond to the charges before the deadline.
According to court documents, Empires Consulting operated a fraudulent investment program, EmpiresX, that falsely promised investors high returns. Pires and Goncalves are accused of taking at least $40 million from victims through false cryptocurrency ads. Instead of investing the funds as promised, the founders abused them to buy Bitcoin and Ethereum, while restricting withdrawals and showing false profits from non-existent investments. The founders of EmpireX used the funds for personal expenses, including luxury goods and travel. Despite this, investigators recovered about $22.8 million in digital currency from them. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: CFTC Orders Over $130 Million Penalty For EmpiresX Founders
data.description:
According to Odaily, the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) announced that a U.S. federal court has ordered the Brazilian founders of the illegal cryptocurrency investment platform EmpiresX to pay over $130 million in fines and restitution. On February 4, Judge Cecilia Altonaga of the Southern District of Florida issued a permanent injunction, economic penalties, and other legal actions against EmpiresX founders Emerson Pires, Flavio Goncalves, and their associate Joshua Nicholas. The case was initially filed on June 30, 2022, and resulted in a default judgment due to the defendants' failure to respond to the charges by the deadline.

Court documents reveal that Empires Consulting operated a fraudulent investment scheme known as EmpiresX, which falsely promised high returns to investors. Pires and Goncalves were accused of obtaining at least $40 million from victims through deceptive cryptocurrency advertisements. Instead of investing these funds as promised, the founders misused them to purchase Bitcoin and Ethereum, restricted withdrawals, and displayed fictitious profits from non-existent investments. The EmpiresX founders used the funds for personal expenses, including luxury purchases and travel. Despite these actions, investigators managed to recover approximately $22.8 million in digital assets from them.
